creator: wolfgang@lyxys.ka.sub.org
text:
(In reply to Vladimir Krstulja from comment #1)
The actual problem I'm seeing is that "service redis stop" does not stop redis, neither does a "kill -TERM". A short search found several people on different operating systems do have this system and a common suggestion was to use SIGKILL.
Now this might be a bug in redis, depending on configuration and/or environment. I'm using redis with unix domain sockets, which appears to be a less common configuration,  and I have redis running in a jail with the jail host being a virtual machine in virtual box.
